subject: Breast
title: Breast Abscess and the 48-Hour Rule
hook: One number decides who gets referred for a breast abscess.

points.heading: Three flavors of mastitis
bullets: Neonatal, lactational and nonlactational are the three types
Neonatal mastitis is a rare Staph aureus infection
Witch's milk is a separate benign newborn finding

points.heading: Lactational infection
bullets: Staph aureus enters through cracked nipples
Milk acts as the perfect culture medium
Marking erythema tracks spread over time

points.heading: Where the pus sits
points.icon: three labeled zones on a breast
bullets: Subareolar, intramammary and retromammary are the three types
Retromammary causes include TB and infected haematoma
Ultrasound shows an ill-defined collection with oedema

points.heading: The 48-hour rule
bullets: No improvement on antibiotics by 48 hours means referral
Needle aspiration is standard for confirmed abscesses
Neonatal abscess needs a small peripheral incision

captionLines: One rule decides who gets referred for a breast abscess. ⏱️
Antibiotics without improvement by 48 hours means urgent referral.
Abscess location, subareolar, intramammary or retromammary, changes the whole workup.
Recall Engine turns any lecture into SBAs, MCQs, flashcards and high-yield notes so mastitis and abscess questions become automatic.
